questions.question: An AI-powered diagnostic tool is able to detect a rare disease with a higher accuracy than human doctors. However, the tool requires access to sensitive patient data. Evaluate the ethical implications of using such a tool in healthcare practice. (20 marks)

A healthcare AI system is designed to autonomously prescribe medication to patients. However, the system has been shown to have a higher rate of incorrect prescriptions compared to human doctors. What are the key ethical considerations that need to be addressed in this scenario? (15 marks)

A medical researcher is working on an AI project to develop a chatbot that can provide patients with emotional support and counseling. However, the chatbot is not subject to the same level of human oversight and quality control as human therapists. What are the potential ethical risks and benefits of using such a chatbot in healthcare practice? (20 marks)
